title: Act 1: Each in Their Own Way

events.text: There was still time before nightfall.

{{characters.soren.name}} spread out the map in front of him, a short stub of charcoal pencil in hand, marking critical junctures. Mark, review from the beginning, mark again. He didn't speak, didn't look at anyone—a machine running at full capacity.

{{characters.elias.name}} sat in another corner of the abandoned space, disassembling his signal receiver. Not because it had any problems—it worked fine. He simply had the habit: with something to take apart and reassemble, his whole being could settle.
He laid the components out on his lap—smallest on the left, largest on the right. Then he started reassembling from the left. Once finished, he'd disassemble again, arrange again, reassemble again.

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: {{characters.soren.name}}, you've already looked at that map—

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: Don't interrupt.
{{characters.lyra.name}} closed her mouth and turned toward {{characters.elias.name}}.

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: {{characters.elias.name}}, is there a problem with that receiver?

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: No.

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: Then why do you keep taking it apart—

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: Habit.

{{characters.lyra.name}} confirmed he didn't plan to say more, then turned her head to look at {{characters.alea.name}}, who was sitting against the wall, looking toward the door, watching with hopeful eyes.
{{characters.alea.name}} felt the gaze and didn't move. She returned a glance from the corner of her eye that communicated one thing clearly: you're looking for the wrong person.
{{characters.lyra.name}} didn't give up.

She rummaged in her pack and pulled out what they'd scavenged today—a bundle of compressed rations, a canister with less than half a bottle of purified water, and a small pack of hard candies found in some black market corner. The wrapper was crumpled, but the seal was still intact.
She walked to {{characters.soren.name}}, placing a ration bar and half a cup of water beside his map—not blocking it, but positioned so he'd see it without looking down.
{{characters.soren.name}} glanced at the ration bar, said nothing, continued studying the map. But he picked it up.
{{characters.lyra.name}} walked to {{characters.elias.name}}, placing another ration beside his leg of arranged components.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: Thanks.

Then she gave {{characters.alea.name}}'s ration to her. {{characters.lyra.name}} opened the candy pack, dispensed four pieces, placing one in front of each person. Then she returned to her position, picked up her own share, and took a bite of her ration.
The abandoned space went quiet.

{{characters.soren.name}} pushed the map aside a bit, picked up his ration, took a bite, continued studying the map. {{characters.elias.name}} set down the receiver, stuffed some ration into his mouth.

{{characters.alea.name}} glanced at what was left by her leg, glanced at {{characters.lyra.name}}—who was seriously eating her own share, not looking her way—then {{characters.alea.name}} picked up her ration.
Four people, in separate corners of that abandoned space, eating their respective shares, no one speaking, waiting for nightfall to arrive.
